summary: Updated the Rust `config-dtk` tool's symbol processing to be more tolerant and more useful when generating `build/GALE01/symbols.txt`. The PR switched duplicate symbol insertion from a hard error to a warning, stopped filtering tree and section symbols only to `.c.o` objects, removed special no-op handling for `.s.o`, inferred zero-sized symbol sizes from the next lower sorted address, emitted optional alignment metadata, and deleted a large block of stale commented-out experimental code. The PR body only says it progresses #1082, and there were no review comments in the slice.
what_changed: In `Context::push_symbol`-style insertion logic, duplicate keys are now detected with `map.get(&key).is_some()` and logged with `warn!` instead of causing `bail!`; the new value is still inserted afterward.

Tree object and section symbol matching no longer requires `tu.ends_with(".c.o")`, so non-`.c.o` translation-unit objects can be processed by `push_tree_symbol`, section size/alignment extraction, and `push_section`.
Removed the explicit `.s.o` match arm that previously ignored assembly object tree nodes and section symbols.
Changed the DTK input/output base path from `config/GALE01` to `build/GALE01`, so generated `splits.txt` and `symbols.txt` are targeted under the build directory.
Reworked symbol sorting: instead of immediately formatting symbols into a `BTreeMap` keyed by address, the code builds `sorted_keys` from `ctx.table_symbols` as address-to-symbol-key entries.
Added a reverse pass over sorted symbol addresses to fill any table symbol with size `0` using `prev_addr - sym.addr`, where `prev_addr` is the next higher symbol address.
When writing `symbols.txt`, the code now warns and skips entries whose table symbol has no corresponding tree symbol rather than unwrapping and panicking.
Symbol output still emits `name = section:0xADDR; // type:... size:... scope:...`, and now appends `align:N` when table symbol alignment is present.
Renamed imported write traits to avoid ambiguity: `std::fmt::Write as FmtWrite` and `std::io::Write as IoWrite`; `write_splits` now uses `FmtWrite`.
Deleted roughly 250 lines of stale commented-out code, including old hardcoded ctor/dtor hacks and abandoned split/symbol writing prototypes.
smart_moves: Using a warning for duplicate symbols makes the generator resilient to imperfect map data while preserving the diagnostic with formatted source-line context.
Sorting by address and walking in reverse is a compact way to infer missing symbol sizes from the next symbol boundary without requiring extra section-table data.
Replacing unchecked paired `unwrap()` access to `tree_symbols` with a warning-and-continue path prevents one missing tree entry from aborting the entire symbols output.
Removing the `.c.o` filter broadens the tool to handle symbols from more object origins instead of silently excluding non-C translation units.
Appending `align` only when available keeps the generated symbol line format stable while preserving useful metadata for symbols that have alignment.
Trait aliasing for `FmtWrite` versus `IoWrite` makes later formatting/writer bounds less ambiguous in Rust code that uses both `write!` on strings and file I/O.
decomp_lessons: Symbol generation tools should be tolerant of partial or duplicate map information: log precise warnings, but keep producing useful output where possible.
When symbol sizes are missing or zero, deriving size from sorted address deltas is a practical decomp tooling tactic, especially for labels or section-table entries with incomplete size metadata.
Avoid over-restricting object-origin filters such as `.c.o` unless there is strong evidence; decomp projects often need the same symbol pipeline to support C, assembly, and generated objects.
Use optional metadata emission for fields like alignment so generated config files gain information without forcing every symbol to have that field.
Prefer removing large commented-out hacks once the real path exists; stale hardcoded ctor/dtor symbol experiments can obscure the current generator behavior.
naming_conventions: The tool uses `tree_symbols` for symbol identity/type/scope information from map tree entries and `table_symbols` for section/address/size/alignment information from section table entries.
The generated symbol format in the diff follows `symbol_name = section:0xADDR; // type:TYPE size:0xSIZE scope:SCOPE` with optional `align:ALIGN` appended.
Rust import aliases `FmtWrite` and `IoWrite` distinguish formatting writes from I/O writes in `tools/config-dtk/src/main.rs`.
Paths in this tool now center generated DTK configuration output under `build/GALE01` rather than `config/GALE01`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The PR removed a `.c.o` suffix guard, allowing `push_tree_symbol` and section-symbol handling to operate on more object-file origins.
The PR removed a special `.s.o` ignore branch, meaning assembly object entries are no longer explicitly discarded in the shown match logic.
The reverse sorted-address pass fills zero-size symbols by subtracting the current symbol address from the next higher address, a useful tactic for producing more complete symbol metadata.
The generator now uses `BTreeMap` ordering by address for deterministic symbol traversal and size inference.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The only body context was `Progresses #1082`; the exact upstream issue details are not included in this slice.
searchable_terms: warn tried to insert existing symbol into the map
follow_up_queries: Look up issue #1082 to understand the broader requested improvements for `config-dtk` symbol generation.
Check subsequent PRs touching `tools/config-dtk/src/main.rs` to see whether `.s.o` handling or inferred symbol sizes needed further fixes.
Search for generated `build/GALE01/symbols.txt` changes after this PR to validate the new `align:` output and size inference behavior.
